name: Inkling
archDisplay: Sparse MoE, 66 layers (975B total / 41B active, 6-of-256 routed experts + 2 shared experts/token), hybrid sliding-window/global attention (5:1), relative positional features (no RoPE)
note: Thinking Machines Lab's (Mira Murati's startup) first public model release — a 975B/41B-active MoE with adjustable inference-time "thinking effort", calibrated uncertainty, and text/vision/audio input.
tpsNote: Official NVFP4 checkpoint needs Blackwell-class GPUs (~600GB VRAM); community Unsloth GGUF quants range 1-bit (~270GB) to 8-bit (~870GB) — far beyond a single RTX 4090 at any quant level.

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, text only (no tools) — the corpus convention for this slug. With-tools variant scores 46.0%.

GDPval-AA v2 Elo-style score; independently corroborated by Artificial Analysis (https://artificialanalysis.ai/articles/thinking-machines-has-released-inkling-the-new-leading-u-s-open-weights-model), which lists the same 1238 figure against Kimi K2.6 (1190) and DeepSeek V4 Flash Max (1189).
BrowseComp with context/browsing tools enabled (the benchmark's standard agentic configuration).
Reported as 'SimpleQA Verified' on the official model card.
MMMU Pro, Standard (10 options) split. The independently-hosted HF blog post reports 73.3%, a minor rounding/transcription discrepancy from the official model card's 73.5% used here.
CharXiv reasoning-question (RQ) subset, no Python tool — matches this corpus's established charxiv convention. With-Python variant scores 82.0%.

Official model card, Agentic/general table, MCP Atlas row (74.1%, xHigh config). Cross-checked column identity via the adjacent GDPVal-AA v2 row matching the corpus's own existing 1238 score exactly. Independently corroborated by Scale AI's official MCP Atlas leaderboard (labs.scale.com/leaderboard/mcp_atlas): 'Inkling (xHigh) — 74.10±2.70'.

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 19/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "Inkling" (Thinking Machines). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via the official announcement (thinkingmachines.ai/news/introducing-inkling/) and model card (thinkingmachines.ai/model-card/inkling/), fetched directly, plus independent press corroboration (TechCrunch, Bloomberg, Axios, SiliconANGLE, MarkTechPost) and third-party benchmark corroboration from Artificial Analysis (which independently placed it at Intelligence Index 41, the highest of any US open-weights model at release, ahead of Nemotron 3 Ultra at 38). This is Thinking Machines Lab's first public model release (their only prior product, Tinker, launched Oct 2025, is a training/fine-tuning API, not a model). Companion model Inkling-Small (276B total/12B active) also announced but not yet added — no dedicated benchmark table found for it separately from Inkling proper. Benchmarks not mapped to an existing taxonomy slug (MCP Atlas, tau3-Banking, AA-Omniscience, Global-MMLU-Lite, Audio MC, MMAU, VoiceBench, FORTRESS, StrongREJECT, ForecastBench, Design Arena) were intentionally left uncaptured rather than force-fit to a non-equivalent existing slug. Pricing reflects Tinker's 64K-context tier post-intro-discount as of 2026-07-17 (tinker-docs.thinkingmachines.ai/tinker/models/); a 256K tier is also offered at roughly 2x the price. VRAM figures per Unsloth's dynamic-quant documentation (unsloth.ai/docs/models/inkling).

2026-07-16: Checked mmlu, math, humaneval, livecodebench, and mmmu (raw, non-Pro) against three source types and found none published for Inkling anywhere. (1) Official materials, all fetched directly: the model card (thinkingmachines.ai/model-card/inkling/), the launch announcement (thinkingmachines.ai/news/introducing-inkling/), and the Hugging Face model README (huggingface.co/thinkingmachines/Inkling) all show the identical benchmark table — Reasoning/Agentic/Factuality/Chat/Vision/Audio/Safety categories — with no MMLU, MATH, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, or MMMU row in any of the three. (2) Independent trackers: Artificial Analysis's full Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown for Inkling (artificialanalysis.ai/models/inkling, browser-rendered) lists all 22 of its tracked evaluations (GDPval-AA v2, τ³-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, AA-Briefcase, AutomationBench-AA, Harvey LAB-AA, EnterpriseOps-Gym-AA, IFBench, APEX-Agents-AA, ITBench-AA, MMMU-Pro, etc.) and none of them are MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/raw-MMMU. Vals.ai's Vals Index for Inkling (49.28% overall, #25/37) is built from SWE-bench Verified, CorpFin v2, Finance Agent v2, and Vibe Code Bench subscores only — same five benchmarks absent. Epoch AI's benchmarking hub does not yet have an Inkling entry as of this date (model released 2026-07-15, one day prior). (3) Sibling cross-reference: no sibling model exists in this corpus (Inkling-Small remains unadded per the existing note) so no comparison table was available there either. This is consistent with the model's already-documented strategy of substituting classic/saturated benchmarks with harder agentic/knowledge evals (GPQA Diamond, AIME 2026, SWE-bench Verified/Pro, GDPval-AA v2, MCP Atlas, CritPt) plus a distinct 'Global-MMLU-Lite' chat-quality metric that is not equivalent to raw MMLU — treating all five gaps as a legitimate, deliberate publication choice rather than a missed data point.

2026-07-17 (round-2 gap chase, mmlu/math/humaneval): Ran a second, deeper pass explicitly avoiding the sources already exhausted on 2026-07-16 (official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM). Checked all 6 newly-assigned source types; none surfaced an MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval number for Inkling. (1) Stanford HELM: fetched crfm.stanford.edu/helm/classic/latest/, /helm/lite/latest/, and /helm/capabilities/latest/ directly, plus a site:crfm.stanford.edu search — no page or leaderboard row mentions Inkling or Thinking Machines Lab. Expected: HELM's model-addition cycle is a curated batch process taking weeks, and Inkling was released only 2 days before this check (2026-07-15 vs. today 2026-07-17), so it would not plausibly appear yet. (2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar: multiple targeted searches ("Inkling Thinking Machines MMLU site:arxiv.org", MATH/HumanEval variants, Semantic Scholar baseline-table search) returned no paper using Inkling as a baseline/comparison point for any of the three benchmarks; the Semantic Scholar API itself rate-limited (HTTP 429) but the arXiv web-search coverage was sufficient to find nothing relevant. (3) GitHub eval-harness logs: found and directly fetched thinking-machines-lab/tinker-cookbook's tinker_cookbook/eval/README.md, which documents the lab's own benchmark harness (supports GSM8K, MATH-500, MMLU-Pro, MMLU-Redux, GPQA, IFEval, MBPP, C-Eval, SuperGPQA, IFBench, AIME 2025/2026, plus experimental LiveCodeBench/Terminal-Bench/SWE-bench). Its 'verified scores' table is for harness-validation only and lists exactly two reference models — Qwen3.5-35B-A3B and GPT-OSS-120B — with no Inkling row and no results.json for Inkling committed anywhere in the repo or found via GitHub-scoped search. (Note: even if found, MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux/MATH-500 are variant benchmarks, not equivalent to this corpus's raw mmlu/math slugs, consistent with the prior round's treatment of Global-MMLU-Lite as non-equivalent.) (4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: checked LiveBench.ai, OpenRouter (per Hacker News discussion of the launch thread, Inkling was not yet listed on OpenRouter or other third-party inference aggregators as of the search date), PapersWithCode (no matching entries), and the LiveCodeBench.github.io leaderboard (no Inkling row) — none had it. Scale AI's SEAL leaderboards were searched but not found to host an MMLU/MATH/HumanEval score for Inkling (Scale AI's MCP Atlas leaderboard, already cited in this file's mcp-atlas result, does not cover these three benchmarks). (5) Non-English sources: searched Chinese (Jiqizhixin/QbitAI-oriented queries) and Japanese-language queries for independently-run MMLU/benchmark numbers; no regional blog or forum post surfaced with a number for any of the three benchmarks. (6) Sibling models: re-confirmed via direct fetch of the Hugging Face launch blog post (huggingface.co/blog/thinkingmachines-inkling) that no comparison table with companion model Inkling-Small (276B/12B-active, still preview-only) includes MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval scores for either sibling; cross-model comparison articles (Sebastian Raschka's architecture/benchmark blog, officechai.com, Latent Space AINews roundup) that tabulate Inkling vs. GLM 5.2 / Kimi K2.6 / Nemotron 3 Ultra all draw exclusively from the same already-known benchmark set (HLE, GPQA, AIME, SWE-bench, Terminal-Bench, IFBench, SimpleQA, BrowseComp) with no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval column. Conclusion: all 6 new source types were genuinely checked and none had mmlu, math, or humaneval for Inkling — consistent with the prior round's finding that this is a deliberate publication gap (the vendor substitutes harder/agentic evals for classic saturated benchmarks) rather than a missed data point. Proposing omitted_no_evidence for all three gaps; no results added.

2026-07-18 (round-3 gap chase, livecodebench + mmmu): Ran a targeted, exhaustive pass on exactly these two current-generation gaps, applying all 6 prescribed methods to each. Conclusion for both: omitted_no_evidence — genuinely unpublished anywhere, not a data-collection lag.

MODALITY CHECK (mmmu): Inkling's own corpus record lists modalities ["text","vision","audio"] and capabilities.vision=true, so raw MMMU is NOT architecturally inapplicable — this model is vision-capable and already has an mmmu-pro score on file. The gap is a genuine reporting gap, not a modality mismatch.

(1) Official materials: Re-fetched the official model card (thinkingmachines.ai/model-card/inkling/) and the Hugging Face model card (huggingface.co/thinkingmachines/Inkling) directly. Both show the identical full benchmark table (Reasoning/Agentic/Factuality/Chat/Vision/Audio/Safety) with no LiveCodeBench row anywhere and only "MMMU Pro (Standard 10): 73.3-73.5%" in the Vision section — no raw/base MMMU row in either source.

(2) Independent trackers — checked six: (a) Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/inkling, fetched directly) lists all ~18 evaluations it tracks for Inkling (GDPval-AA v2, τ³-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, AA-Briefcase, AutomationBench-AA, Harvey LAB-AA, EnterpriseOps-Gym-AA, IFBench, APEX-Agents-AA, ITBench-AA, MMMU-Pro) — no LiveCodeBench, no raw MMMU. (b) Vals.ai's Vals Index composition for Inkling (SWE-bench Verified, CorpFin v2, Finance Agent v2, Vibe Code Bench) excludes both. (c) BenchLM.ai's Inkling model page (benchlm.ai/models/inkling, fetched directly) explicitly states "LiveCodeBench and base MMMU scores are not displayed on this page" among its 33 tracked benchmarks. (d) llm-stats.com's dedicated LiveCodeBench v6 leaderboard (53 models) and dedicated MMMU leaderboard (63 models) were each fetched directly — Inkling does not appear on either. (e) BenchLM's own LiveCodeBench v6 leaderboard (10 models) also excludes Inkling. (f) LMArena/arena.ai's text arena (arena.ai/leaderboard) does list Inkling at #67 overall with a "Coding" arena Elo-style score of 73 — but this is a human-preference arena score, not the fixed-problem-set LiveCodeBench pass-rate this corpus's livecodebench slug tracks (confirmed against corpus/benchmarks.json: livecodebench is a 0-94% pass-rate metric, category=coding), so it is not usable as a substitute. Scale AI's SEAL leaderboards were checked (labs.scale.com/leaderboard) with no evidence of LiveCodeBench or MMMU coverage for Inkling beyond the already-captured MCP Atlas entry.

(3) Academic sources: Targeted arXiv/web searches ("Inkling 975B baseline LiveCodeBench evaluation 2026", MMMU variants) returned no paper using Inkling as a baseline/comparison point for either benchmark.

(4) GitHub eval-harness: Re-confirmed via search that thinking-machines-lab/tinker-cookbook lists LiveCodeBench only as "experimental" support (alongside Terminal-Bench and SWE-bench), with no committed results.json or verified score for Inkling; no MMMU harness support found at all in this repo.

(5) Sibling-model comparison: Checked comparison articles/tables for GLM-5.2, Kimi K2.6, and Nemotron 3 Ultra (BenchLM's glm-5-2-vs-inkling page, Sebastian Raschka's architecture/benchmark blog post fetched directly, Latent Space AINews roundup, officechai.com, kingy.ai) — all draw from the same known benchmark set (HLE, GPQA, AIME, SWE-bench, Terminal-Bench, IFBench, SimpleQA, MMMU-Pro, FORTRESS, etc.); none tabulate LiveCodeBench or raw MMMU for Inkling.

(6) Non-English sources: Fetched a detailed Japanese-language technical writeup (note.com/humble_bobcat51, full benchmark table reproduced) directly — confirms the same benchmark set with MMMU Pro only, no raw MMMU, no LiveCodeBench.

Conclusion: both gaps checked across all 6 methods; neither benchmark has been published for Inkling by any source found. This appears to be a genuine, deliberate vendor omission (consistent with Inkling's documented pattern of substituting MMMU-Pro for raw MMMU and omitting classic saturated-style benchmarks) rather than a "not yet indexed" lag — independent trackers (AA, Vals, BenchLM, llm-stats) all already have live, populated pages for Inkling covering 20-30+ other benchmarks each within 3 days of release, so the absence specifically of LiveCodeBench and MMMU across all of them is unlikely to be simple lag. Proposing omitted_no_evidence for both; no results added.
